steps.title: Identify the explicit contrast in the passage
steps.content: The passage states Whitten's process was a “clear departure from the prevalence of gestures in his work from the 1960s.” This directly establishes that gestures were plentiful in his 1960s work.

steps.title: Determine what both parties would agree on
steps.content:
B captures what both would agree on: since Whitten emphasizes departing from the “prevalence” of gestures in his 1960s work, and the art historians see the 1970s shift as “removing gesture,” they would both acknowledge that 1960s work had more gestures than 1970s work.

A over-generalizes beyond the passage; C infers unsupported motivation; D discusses unmentioned realism.

This matches choice B — B is correct.
